claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits) and label-heavy structure reflect a fixed-vocabulary encoding in which each label token is drawn from a closed set of fewer than 50 distinct label templates, with high within-label glyph redundancy serving as a positional marker rather than encoding phonemic content.
evidence_for: Zodiac section has fewest folios (12) and words (1,297) relative to other sections, consistent with a label-only register
Lowest section entropy (3.7149 bits) suggests reduced glyph diversity, compatible with a closed label vocabulary
Astronomical section also has low entropy (3.7471 bits) and is similarly diagram-heavy, supporting a diagram-label encoding regime
evidence_against: If labels use a closed template set, type-token ratio for the zodiac section should be markedly lower than other sections; this is testable but not yet confirmed
Low entropy could alternatively reflect a denser homophonic encoding rather than a restricted vocabulary
test: Compute type-token ratio (unique words / total words) for each section; test whether zodiac and astronomical sections have significantly lower TTR than herbal, recipes, and text-only sections using a one-sided permutation test
test_metric: Type-token ratio per section; permutation test p-value comparing label-heavy vs. prose-heavy sections
test_threshold: Zodiac TTR < 0.30 and permutation p < 0.05 relative to herbal/recipes/text-only sections would confirm closed-vocabulary label encoding; TTR comparable to other sections would refute it

tests_run.test: compute type-token ratio (unique words / total words) for each section; test whether zodiac and astr
tests_run.details: Section entropy std dev: 0.0593. Range: 3.7149 - 3.9016. Variable across sections.

reasoning: If zodiac labels are drawn from a closed template set, the section should show a markedly lower TTR than prose sections. Confirming this would establish a two-register model (label vs. prose) that constrains decipherment strategies differently for each section.
